title: systemd --user session fails due to permissions on /sys/fs/cgroup/systemd
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

"Interactive" sessions for non-root users in systemd container do not function properly due to systemctl -session failing, which fails most of modern functionality, including dbus, ssh sessions, etc

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. create container and enable systemd-logind 
```
sudo podman rm -vf sysuser; sudo podman run -it -d --name sysuser fedora:30 /sbin/init
sudo podman exec -it sysuser bash -c "systemctl unmask systemd-logind --now; useradd user; systemctl status;"

sudo podman exec -it sysuser bash -c "rpm -q fedora-release-container systemd"
fedora-release-container-30-3.noarch
systemd-241-8.git9ef65cb.fc30.x86_64
```
2. The issue is observed when logging as user
```
$ sudo podman exec -it sysuser su - user -c 'bash -c "id; sleep 2; SYSTEMD_LOG_LEVEL=debug systemctl --user"'
uid=1000(user) gid=1000(user) groups=1000(user)
running_in_chroot(): Permission denied
Bus n/a: changing state UNSET → OPENING
Bus n/a: changing state OPENING → CLOSED
Bus n/a: changing state UNSET → OPENING
Bus n/a: changing state OPENING → CLOSED
Failed to connect to bus: No such file or directory
```
3. "user session" service `systemctl status user@1000.service` fails, specifically:
```
$ sudo podman exec -it sysuser su - user -c 'bash -c "id; sleep 2; SYSTEMD_LOG_LEVEL=debug /usr/lib/systemd/systemd --user"'
uid=1000(user) gid=1000(user) groups=1000(user)
systemd v241-8.git9ef65cb.fc30 running in user mode for user 1000/user. (+PAM +AUDIT +SELINUX +IMA -APPARMOR +SMACK +SYSVINIT +UTMP +LIBCRYPTSETUP +GCRYPT +GNUTLS +ACL +XZ +LZ4 +SECCOMP +BLKID +ELFUTILS +KMOD +IDN2 -IDN +PCRE2 default-hierarchy=hybrid)
Found cgroup on /sys/fs/cgroup/systemd, legacy hierarchy
Using cgroup controller name=systemd. File system hierarchy is at /sys/fs/cgroup/systemd/machine.slice/libpod-4094331415fc900b25e291937839d2ea1ca74288023215bf4f3f488fc075d6a9.scope/machine.slice/libpod-4094331415fc900b25e291937839d2ea1ca74288023215bf4f3f488fc075d6a9.scope/user.slice/user-1000.slice/session-1.scope.
Failed to create /machine.slice/libpod-4094331415fc900b25e291937839d2ea1ca74288023215bf4f3f488fc075d6a9.scope/machine.slice/libpod-4094331415fc900b25e291937839d2ea1ca74288023215bf4f3f488fc075d6a9.scope/user.slice/user-1000.slice/session-1.scope/init.scope control group: Permission denied
Failed to allocate manager object: Permission denied

```

4. I have found a brutal workaround, could not pinpoint it any closer. Allow writes for everyone to /sys/fs/cgroup/systemd/ in container:
```
sudo podman exec -it sysuser bash -c "chmod o+rwx -R /sys/fs/cgroup/systemd/"
```
now user session will starte successfully upon user login (you might need to increase sleep to allow user@1000.service to start , ie:
```
sudo podman exec -it sysuser su - user -c 'bash -c "id; sleep 2; SYSTEMD_LOG_LEVEL=debug systemctl --user"'
uid=1000(user) gid=1000(user) groups=1000(user)
running_in_chroot(): Permission denied
Bus n/a: changing state UNSET → OPENING
Bus n/a: changing state OPENING → AUTHENTICATING
Successfully forked off '(pager)' as PID 246.

Got message type=method_return sender=org.freedesktop.systemd1 destination=n/a path=n/a interface=n/a member=n/a cookie=1 reply_cookie=1 signature=a(ssssssouso) error-name=n/a error-message=n
```

**Describe the results you received:**
"Interactive" sessions for non-root users in systemd container fail

**Describe the results you expected:**
"Functional" user accounts inside non-privileged podman systemd containers

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**
```
$ podman version
Version:            1.4.2
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.11.10
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
physical

Same issue happens in f29 containers with systemd-239-12.git8bca462.fc29.x86_64

comments.body:
```
/machine.slice/libpod-4094331415fc900b25e291937839d2ea1ca74288023215bf4f3f488fc075d6a9.scope/machine.slice/libpod-4094331415fc900b25e291937839d2ea1ca74288023215bf4f3f488fc075d6a9.scope/user.slice/user-1000.slice/session-1.scope/init.scope
```

This path doesn't look correct - it seems like we've duplicated the `/machine.slice/libpod-$CID.scope` portion? Could be related.
Output of `systemd-cgls` on the host with the systemd container running might help debug

I was "holding" a user session using `sudo podman exec -it sysuser su - user -c 'bash -c "id; sleep 3; SYSTEMD_LOG_LEVEL=debug systemctl --user; sleep 60"'`, here is the subset of the results for the given container:
```
  ├─libpod-conmon-238fc4af93193ab68cb8758f2ac1f043f66d576abc42fb507b6ffcb0137567cb.scope
  │ └─3617 /usr/libexec/podman/conmon -s -c 238fc4af93193ab68cb8758f2ac1f043f...
  ├─libpod-238fc4af93193ab68cb8758f2ac1f043f66d576abc42fb507b6ffcb0137567cb.scope
  │ ├─3629 /sbin/init
  │ ├─3660 /usr/lib/systemd/systemd-journald
  │ ├─3738 /usr/bin/dbus-broker-launch --scope system --audit
  │ ├─3739 dbus-broker --log 4 --controller 9 --machine-id f75b8364c0bf4b2ca7...
  │ └─3800 /usr/lib/systemd/systemd-logind
```

I am not sure it will work without properly creating the user session, if you try to use directly `systemctl --user` on the host you'll probably get the same error.

Could you try to run as root `systemctl start user@1000.service` or alternatively `loginctl enable-linger 1000`?
the user session creation is enabled by unmasking `systemd-logind`. `systemctl status user@1000.service` has the same output as my step 3 - it attempts to start and fails without the chmod.
Aren't we enabling linger by default now?

> Aren't we enabling linger by default now?

yes, but the issue seems to be about running the container as root user and inside of the container create the user session.

Seems to work for me now with: https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/3731/files

@paravz could you try with that PR?
is there a way to get the built binary (or rpm) from CI ? I want to avoid my build environment introducing something
@paravz We released podman 1.5 yesterday with this fix, It should be available soon in Fedora soon
podman from https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/3731/files fixes this issue indeed. Thank you!
@mheon same scenario is broken in pre-release Fedora 32, likely in systemd: https://github.com/systemd/systemd/issues/15408
